evals.prompt: /project:data-science-tutorial double machine learning dataset: DS4Bolivia references: https://docs.doubleml.org/stable/intro/intro.html
evals.expected_output: Creates code/tut_doubleml.py and notebooks/notebook-NN.ipynb + .md with a DoubleML case-study tutorial using DS4Bolivia data. Fetches DoubleML docs to inform implementation. Frames as a causal inference case study: does satellite-observed urbanization causally affect municipal development? Includes PLR or IRM model, cross-fitting visualization, treatment effect estimation, and interpretation cells. References section includes DoubleML docs and DS4Bolivia.

assertions.text: Script code/tut_doubleml.py exists and is runnable with uv run python
Script imports from config.py and calls set_seeds()
Script uses exact DS4Bolivia raw GitHub URLs (master branch) with local caching
Notebook .md uses correct MyST Jupytext frontmatter with formats: ipynb,md:myst
Colab badge URL points to correct notebook-NN.ipynb path
First code cell detects google.colab and clones repo
Imports from config.py and calls set_seeds() in notebook
Loads data from DS4Bolivia with caching to data/rawData/
Uses doubleml library (DoubleMLPLR or DoubleMLIRM) for causal inference
Case study introduction motivates a causal question about Bolivian municipalities
All plt.savefig() calls target images/ directory with tut_doubleml_ prefix
At least 3 figures saved to images/
Figure cells use #| label: fig-* directives
Single-line markdown paragraphs (no hard line breaks within paragraphs)
Notebook contains interpretation markdown after at least 5 code cells referencing specific numeric output values
References section at end includes DoubleML docs URL and DS4Bolivia repository URL
Learning objectives listed as bullet points

evals.expected_output: Creates code/tut_kmeans.py and notebooks/notebook-NN.ipynb + .md with a k-means clustering case-study tutorial using DS4Bolivia satellite embeddings. Frames as: can we identify distinct groups of municipalities based on satellite imagery? Includes elbow/silhouette analysis, cluster visualization, and profile interpretation.

assertions.text: Script code/tut_kmeans.py exists and is runnable
Script imports from config.py and calls set_seeds()
Uses DS4Bolivia data with A00-A63 satellite embeddings as clustering features
Notebook .md uses correct MyST Jupytext frontmatter
Colab badge and setup cell present and correct
Implements KMeans from scikit-learn
Includes at least one method for choosing number of clusters (elbow or silhouette)
Case study introduction motivates grouping Bolivian municipalities
All plt.savefig() calls target images/ directory with tut_kmeans_ prefix
At least 3 figures saved to images/
Figure cells use #| label: fig-* directives
Single-line markdown paragraphs
Notebook contains interpretation markdown after at least 5 code cells referencing specific numeric output values
References section at end includes DS4Bolivia repository URL
No doubleml or other unrelated libraries imported

evals.prompt: /project:data-science-tutorial gradient boosting regression dataset: DS4Bolivia references: https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/ensemble.html#gradient-boosting
evals.expected_output: Creates code/tut_gradient_boosting.py and notebooks/notebook-NN.ipynb + .md with a gradient boosting case-study tutorial predicting IMDS from satellite embeddings. Frames as: can gradient boosting outperform Random Forest for predicting municipal development? Includes learning curves, feature importance, and comparison metrics.

assertions.text: Script code/tut_gradient_boosting.py exists and is runnable
Script imports from config.py and calls set_seeds()
Uses imds as target variable and A00-A63 as features
Notebook .md uses correct MyST Jupytext frontmatter
Colab badge and setup cell present and correct
Implements GradientBoostingRegressor or HistGradientBoostingRegressor from scikit-learn
Uses train_test_split with test_size=0.2 and random_state=RANDOM_SEED
Case study introduction frames a clear predictive question
All plt.savefig() calls target images/ directory with tut_gradient_boosting_ prefix
At least 3 figures saved to images/
Includes evaluation metrics (R², RMSE, MAE)
Single-line markdown paragraphs
Notebook contains interpretation markdown after at least 5 code cells referencing specific numeric values
References section at end includes scikit-learn docs URL and DS4Bolivia repository URL
Learning objectives listed as bullet points
